title: US stock market today hits new records as Nasdaq crossed 25,000 for the very first time: Dow Jones, S&P 50
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The Nasdaq Composite reached 25,000 for the first time, while the Dow Jones and S&P 500 also hit record highs, driven by strong earnings and market optimism. Ap
excerpt: The Nasdaq Composite reached 25,000 for the first time, while the Dow Jones and S&P 500 also hit record highs, driven by strong earnings and market optimism. Apple led gains, contributing to the surge.

content.introduction: The Nasdaq Composite reached 25,000 for the first time, while the Dow Jones and S&P 500 also hit record highs, driven by strong earnings and market optimism. Apple led gains, contributing to the surge. Nasdaq ends above 25,000 for first time ever as stocks kick off May with records ...
body:
## Market Overview

The stock has hit a new all-time high. NDX 0.94% US500 The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 115.00 to 48,578.72, and the Nasdaq composite gained 86.69 to 24,102.70. In stock markets abroad, indexes Stock indexes settled mostly higher on Friday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 posting new all-time highs. Intel rose more than 23% to a record Invest Now (https: www.motilaloswalmf.com mutual-funds motilal-oswal-midcap-fund?utm campaign midcap&utm source et&utm medium invest now cta&utm creative cpc&utm device paid) The Nasdaq Composite reached 25,000 for the first time, while the Dow Jones and S&P 500 also hit record highs, driven by strong earnings and market optimism. Apple led gains, contributing to the surge. The Economic Times The Economic Times daily newspaper is available online now. US stock market today hits new records as Nasdaq crossed 25,000 for the very first time: Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq surge as Wall Street begins May with massive rally - Apple leads gains Google Logo Whatsapp Follow Channel US stock market today opens May with rare strength. The Dow Jones Industrial Average climbs near 49,815. The S&P 500 hits fresh highs above 7,255. The Nasdaq crossed 25,000 for the very first time a threshold that would have seemed impossible just months ago when AI concerns rattled software stocks. The US stock market today is not just recovering. It is rewriting its own history, one record close at a time. Life lessons on violence, negotiation, patience and why is it beneficial to show restraint than taking impulsive action in conflicts:Image Quote of the day by Bruce Lee: 'If you spend too much time thinking about a thing, you'll never...' - inspiring lessons on why overthinking stops progress and action creates success by Enter the Dragon and Fist of Fury actor:Image ... African proverb of the day: A woman holds the knife at the sharp end Life lessons on emotional strength, social behavior and sacrifice:Image Quote of the day by Marlon Brando: 'I don't see anybody as evil.

## Key Developments

When you start seeing people as evil, you're in...' - life lessons on empathy, non-judgemental attitude and how to understand people better by Oscar-winning actor of On the Waterfront and Godfather:Image The Economic Times The Economic Times As of 10:05 a.m., the Dow was up 325.21 points at 49,977.35. The index had momentarily surged more than 730 points in the previous session after Caterpillar (CAT) reported blowout earnings, and that momentum carried firmly into Friday's trading. The Nasdaq Composite was up 251.50 points at 25,143.81, hitting a new all-time high shortly after the opening bell. The push above the 25,000 threshold had been viewed by market participants as a key psychological milestone. Among individual stocks, Apple (AAPL) surged more than 5%, leading the Dow higher after its January-to-March quarter results showed strong sales of its flagship iPhone. Conversely, Amgen (AMGN), which had been bought up the previous day, fell over 3%. Profit-taking appeared to hit some defensive names that had previously supported the market. Following the data release, US Treasuries rallied in the bond market, pushing the benchmark 10-year yield down from 4.402% to 4.3408%. In currency markets, the dollar was sold off, with the dollar-yen pair struggling around the mid-156 range. The euro initially weakened to $1.1742 before climbing to $1.1785. The S&P on Thursday posted its best monthly increase since November 2020, advancing 10.4% for April. The Nasdaq jumped 15.3% for its best month Wednesday s session is being written in real time as one of the defining trading days of 2026, a session where the arithmetic of geopolitics, corporate earnings, and Federal Reserve politics are colliding simultaneously on a tape that is straining toward record territory. The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading at 6,994.95, separated from its all-time intraday high of 7,002.28 set on January 28 by fewer than eight points. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP) is extending a winning streak to 11 consecutive sessions, its longest run since November 2021, up 227 points or 1% to 23,867.

## What Investors Should Watch

The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), carrying the weight of its more cyclical and rate-sensitive composition, is lagging the entire tape, shedding 192 points or 0.40% to 48,343. The Russell 2000 is essentially flat, up ...
